upper-east-side.description: Late-40s, two kids in private school, dual-income (finance + nonprofit). Doorman building, classic six. Wants top schools, polished feel, established neighbors. Walking distance to the Met and to a real grocery. Less interested in scene than in stability.

west-village.description: Late-30s, professional, partner, no kids in the immediate plan. Two-bedroom prewar walk-up, exposed brick, original moldings. Wants the village texture (cobblestones, jazz, the right cafés) and walks to a different world-class restaurant on Friday nights. Pays for it.

park-slope.description: Mid-30s couple, one kid in pre-K. Walk to the park, walk to the food coop. Subaru in the driveway for weekend hikes. Wants brownstone block and good schools more than Manhattan access. Reads NYT. Trades trendy for emotional sustainability.

bushwick.description: Late-20s, partner, no kids. Graphic designer + freelance writer. Loft floor-share, exposed pipes, big windows. Friday gallery openings, late-night pizza at Roberta's, Saturday brunch at Ops. Wants the scene at the doorstep, not as a destination.

astoria.description: Late-20s to mid-30s, partner or married, no kids yet. Modest one-bedroom in a prewar walk-up. Wants the food (Greek, Egyptian, Brazilian, Bengali — all on one block), the price, the N/W to Midtown. Brunch in Manhattan on Saturdays, dinner in Astoria the rest of the week.

larchmont.description: Mid-30s, two kids, dog, station wagon. Train to Manhattan three days a week. Wants the walkable downtown grocery, the tree-lined blocks, the Westchester schools — but doesn't want a car-only suburb. Specifically picked Larchmont over Scarsdale for the walkable village.
